hub_subtitle: Business English para Missões Internacionais e Pequenos Negócios

lesson.menu_title: The Team — What You and Your Colleagues Do
lesson.menu_desc: Describe your team and your weekly routine with adverbs of frequency -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 2 -- The Team: What You and Your Colleagues Do
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Flávia Florêncio Ferreira | Lesson 2 -- The Team

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: the final day to finish a task
to make something grow and get better
a plan of times and dates for your work
the place where you work at a desk
a duty that is yours to do
to arrange things in a clear order
a piece of work you have to do
something that happens one time every week
to decide the steps before you do something

paras: Flavia works with a small team at Sebrae in Sao Paulo. The team always starts the week with a meeting on Monday. In this meeting, they plan the projects and organize the tasks. Every colleague gets a task and a deadline for the week.

Flavia usually works at the office, but she often works from home too. She always checks her schedule in the morning. Her team sometimes visits small companies in the region, and they never miss an important deadline. Training the colleagues is Flavia's main responsibility.

To say how often you do something, use an adverb of frequency: always (100%), usually, often, sometimes, and never (0%). The adverb goes before the main verb: 'We always meet on Monday.' But it goes after the verb 'to be': 'She is always ready.' Never say 'We meet always.'
reading.source: Alumni by Better — A2 reading about a work routine

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Flavia works alone and never meets her team.
Flavia's team has a weekly routine, and we use adverbs of frequency to describe it.
The text explains how to start a small company in Sao Paulo.

items: The team always meets on Monday.
The text says: 'The team always starts the week with a meeting on Monday.'
The text says: 'she often works from home too.'
Every colleague gets a task and a deadline.
The text says: 'Every colleague gets a task and a deadline for the week.'
The team often misses important deadlines.
The text says: 'they never miss an important deadline.'

parts: meets to plan the week. Flavia
works at the office, and she

gapfill.text: Remember: the adverb goes BEFORE the main verb (I always plan), but AFTER 'to be' (I am always busy). Order: always, usually, often, sometimes, never.

bank.label: Useful sentences to describe your team and your week
items: We always meet on Monday to plan the week.
I usually work at the office.
I often work from home too.
Each colleague has a task and a deadline.
Training my colleagues is my responsibility.
We sometimes develop new ideas for small companies.

lf.text: Before a main verb, the adverb comes first (always meet, usually work). With the verb 'to be', it comes after (is always, are usually).

items.situation: Say how often your team meets. Use an adverb of frequency.
tips: My team usually meets once a week.

items.situation: Describe where you work during the week.
tips: I usually work at the office.

items.situation: Talk about deadlines. How reliable are you?
tips: I am always ready on time.

items.situation: Describe one responsibility you have on your team.
tips: Training my colleagues is my responsibility.

items: Describe your team's week in three sentences: when you meet, what you do, and who organizes the schedule. Use your notes.
Say it again, and add two adverbs of frequency: 'We always meet on Monday' and 'I sometimes work from home.'
Now do it with no notes. Sound natural, not memorized.
A colleague asks: 'Do you ever miss a deadline?' Answer: 'No, I never miss a deadline. I am always ready.'

answerkey.title: Reveal the model description
list: Model: 'I work with a small team at Sebrae. We always meet on Monday to plan the week. Each colleague has a task and a deadline. I usually work at the office, but I often work from home. I always organize the schedule, and I never miss a deadline.'
Adverb before the main verb: I always plan, we usually meet, she never misses.
Adverb after the verb to be: I am always ready, she is usually busy.
Order of frequency: always (100%), usually, often, sometimes, never (0%).
answerkey.note: The adverb goes before the main verb, but after am / is / are.

listenings.text: Hi Flavia, this is Bruno from the projects team. Here is the plan for the week. On Monday, we always meet at nine to organize the tasks. On Tuesday and Wednesday, the team usually works at the office. On Thursday, we often visit small companies in the region. On Friday, we sometimes review the results before the deadline. Please check your schedule and confirm the meeting. Thank you, and have a good week.

Good morning, everyone. This is a quick reminder about our weekly meeting. We always start on Monday at nine, so please be on time. Bring your task list and your deadlines for the week. Marina usually shares the schedule, and she is always ready to help. Remember, we never cancel the Monday meeting, because it is important to plan the week together. See you all soon, and thank you.

extra_audio.text: On Monday, the team always meets to plan the week. Each colleague gets a task and a deadline. During the week, they usually organize the projects together. On Wednesday, they often check the schedule and the progress. On Friday, they sometimes review the results before the deadline.
